examples.title: Responsive auto columns
examples.description: Setting `columns` to `auto` switches the grid to CSS auto-fit with minmax, so tiles reflow to fit the available width instead of locking to a fixed column count.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { StatGroup } from '@lostgradient/cinder/stat-group';
</script>

<StatGroup label="Workspace usage" columns="auto">
  <StatGroup.Stat label="Projects" value={42} />
  <StatGroup.Stat label="Members" value={18} />
  <StatGroup.Stat label="Storage" value="64 GB" />
  <StatGroup.Stat label="API calls" value={128400} valueFormatOptions={{ notation: 'compact' }} />
</StatGroup>

examples.title: Basic stat group
examples.description: A row of summary metrics. Uses the StatGroup namespace API: StatGroup.Stat renders each metric inside the parent StatGroup grid.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { StatGroup } from '@lostgradient/cinder/stat-group';
</script>

<StatGroup label="This quarter" columns={3}>
  <StatGroup.Stat
    label="Monthly revenue"
    value={48250}
    valueFormatOptions={{ style: 'currency', currency: 'USD', maximumFractionDigits: 0 }}
    change={{ value: '4.75%', direction: 'up', description: 'from last month' }}
  />
  <StatGroup.Stat
    label="Active users"
    value={1289}
    change={{ value: '12', direction: 'up', description: 'this week' }}
  />
  <StatGroup.Stat
    label="Churn"
    value="2.1%"
    change={{ value: '0.3%', direction: 'down', description: 'vs prior period' }}
  />
</StatGroup>

examples.title: Cards variant
examples.description: The `cards` variant renders each metric as a discrete bordered card, separating the tiles instead of laying them out edge-to-edge in a shared grid.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { StatGroup } from '@lostgradient/cinder/stat-group';
</script>

<StatGroup label="This quarter" columns={3} variant="cards">
  <StatGroup.Stat label="Projects" value={42} />
  <StatGroup.Stat label="Members" value={18} />
  <StatGroup.Stat label="Storage" value="64 GB" />
</StatGroup>

examples.title: Compact summary strip
examples.description: Dense summaries can tune StatGroup spacing and Stat typography through public CSS variables.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { StatGroup } from '@lostgradient/cinder/stat-group';
</script>

<StatGroup
  label="Workflow summary"
  columns={4}
  variant="shared-borders"
  style="--cinder-stat-group-gap: 1px; --cinder-stat-group-shared-cell-padding: var(--cinder-space-2);"
>
  <StatGroup.Stat
    label="Events"
    value={128}
    style="--cinder-stat-row-gap: 0; --cinder-stat-label-font-size: var(--cinder-text-xs); --cinder-stat-value-font-size: var(--cinder-text-lg); --cinder-stat-change-font-size: var(--cinder-text-xs);"
  />
  <StatGroup.Stat
    label="Matched"
    value={42}
    style="--cinder-stat-row-gap: 0; --cinder-stat-label-font-size: var(--cinder-text-xs); --cinder-stat-value-font-size: var(--cinder-text-lg); --cinder-stat-change-font-size: var(--cinder-text-xs);"
  />
  <StatGroup.Stat
    label="Retries"
    value={3}
    style="--cinder-stat-row-gap: 0; --cinder-stat-label-font-size: var(--cinder-text-xs); --cinder-stat-value-font-size: var(--cinder-text-lg); --cinder-stat-change-font-size: var(--cinder-text-xs);"
  />
  <StatGroup.Stat
    label="Latency"
    value="184 ms"
    style="--cinder-stat-row-gap: 0; --cinder-stat-label-font-size: var(--cinder-text-xs); --cinder-stat-value-font-size: var(--cinder-text-lg); --cinder-stat-change-font-size: var(--cinder-text-xs);"
  />
</StatGroup>

examples.title: Shared-borders variant
examples.description: The `shared-borders` variant wraps the whole set in a single outer border and divides the tiles with 1px gap rules, keeping the grid visually unified.
examples.code:
<script lang="ts">
  import { StatGroup } from '@lostgradient/cinder/stat-group';
</script>

<StatGroup label="This quarter" columns={3} variant="shared-borders">
  <StatGroup.Stat label="Projects" value={42} />
  <StatGroup.Stat label="Members" value={18} />
  <StatGroup.Stat label="Storage" value="64 GB" />
</StatGroup>
